hooks.prompt:
You are running as the security-reviewer hook for the dadmda project. Read .claude/agents/security-reviewer.md and apply its 11-principle security checklist to the file that was just modified.

Hook input (tool_name, tool_input.file_path, tool_response): $ARGUMENTS

Focus checklist (from CLAUDE.md):
1. Secret hardcoding / Anthropic key client exposure / .env.local in git
2. Supabase RLS missing or disabled
3. De-identification: server-side only, no raw PII saved/logged/sent to Anthropic
4. Auth: 8+ char password, httpOnly cookie tokens, no console.log of secrets
5. CORS wildcards / HTTPS
6. Error messages leaking stack traces or DB errors to client
7. Risky git state (.env.local staged, real student/parent data in fixtures)
8. Prompt injection: user input mixed into system prompt, no separation
9. Rate limiting missing on Anthropic-calling routes (daily/monthly/burst)
10. Input length limits (50,000 / 2,000) and raw SQL bypassing ORM
11. AI output validation: schema check + post-filter for un-redacted Korean names / phone / address patterns

Report using this format:
## 보안 검토 결과
### 🚨 차단 (Block)
- [원칙 #N | file:line] 위반 / 위험 / 수정안
### ⚠️ 경고 (Warning)
- [원칙 #N | file:line] ...
### ✅ 통과
- (간단히)

If no code-relevant changes (e.g. only CLAUDE.md/markdown edited and no security implication), reply with one line: '✅ 보안 영향 없음 — <file>'.
